metadata.title: Mathematics Expansion and Test Coverage
metadata.description: Expands the mathematics branch with additional foundational nodes and upgrades every mathematics node to a 10-question mastery test.
metadata.author: Knowledge Nexus

nodes.title: Number Line and Integers
nodes.subject: Mathematics
nodes.description: The number line organizes positive numbers, negative numbers, and zero so learners can reason about order, distance, and direction.
nodes.why_it_matters: It builds intuition for signed quantities, comparison, and the algebraic idea of moving values left and right through operations.
use_cases: Reading elevations above and below sea level

nodes.title: Order of Operations
nodes.subject: Mathematics
nodes.description: Order of operations gives a shared rule set for evaluating expressions with parentheses, exponents, multiplication, division, addition, and subtraction.
nodes.why_it_matters: Without it, the same expression could produce different answers. It is a direct bridge from arithmetic into algebraic thinking.

nodes.title: Decimals and Percentages
nodes.subject: Mathematics
nodes.description: Decimals and percentages express parts of a whole in forms commonly used for money, measurement, probability, and data summaries.
nodes.why_it_matters: They connect arithmetic, fractions, ratios, and real-world interpretation in a way learners use every day.

nodes.title: Coordinate Geometry
nodes.subject: Mathematics
nodes.description: Coordinate geometry studies points, lines, and shapes using algebra on the coordinate plane.
nodes.why_it_matters: It is where graphical intuition and symbolic manipulation meet, preparing learners for functions, vectors, and analytic modeling.

nodes.title: Polynomials
nodes.subject: Mathematics
nodes.description: Polynomials combine powers of variables into expressions that can be simplified, graphed, factored, and used to model changing quantities.
nodes.why_it_matters: They are central in algebra, functions, calculus preparation, and many scientific models.

tests.title: Basic Arithmetic Mastery Test
tests.instructions: Answer questions about the core concepts, interpretations, and applications for this node.

questions.prompt: Which arithmetic operation combines two quantities into a total?
Which operation tells you how much remains after removing one amount from another?
What is multiplication best described as in basic arithmetic?
What is 24 divided by 6?
Which operation is the inverse of addition?
What is 7 + 8?
If you have 3 groups of 5 objects, how many objects do you have in total?
What is the name for the answer to a division problem?
Which everyday task depends directly on basic arithmetic?
Why is basic arithmetic foundational in the graph?

options.option_text: It supports nearly every later math topic
It is mainly a statistics topic

tests.title: Fractions Ratios Mastery Test
tests.instructions: Answer questions about the core concepts, interpretations, and applications for this node.

questions.prompt: In the fraction 3/4, what is the number 3 called?
In the fraction 3/4, what is the number 4 called?
What is 1/2 + 1/4?
Which fraction is equivalent to 2/3?
What is the simplified form of the ratio 6:3?
Which idea does a fraction represent?

options.option_text: A point on a graph only

questions.prompt: Which fraction is equal to 25 percent?
Why are ratios useful in real life?

options.option_text: They compare quantities like ingredients or scale
They remove the need for division

questions.prompt: What is a good first step when comparing 2/5 and 3/10?

options.option_text: Rewrite them with a common denominator

questions.prompt: What does a unit rate compare?

options.option_text: An amount per 1 unit of another quantity

tests.title: Basic Geometry Mastery Test
tests.instructions: Answer questions about the core concepts, interpretations, and applications for this node.

questions.prompt: What is an angle greater than 90 degrees but less than 180 degrees called?
How do you find the area of a rectangle?
How do you find the perimeter of a triangle?

options.option_text: Add the lengths of all three sides

questions.prompt: How many degrees are around a full point?
What is the boundary of a circle called?
Which formula gives the volume of a rectangular prism?
What is true about parallel lines in Euclidean geometry?

options.option_text: They always meet at right angles

questions.prompt: How many degrees are in a right angle?
What is the sum of the interior angles of a triangle?
Which field depends heavily on basic geometry?

tests.title: Algebra Mastery Test
tests.instructions: Answer questions about the core concepts, interpretations, and applications for this node.

questions.prompt: What does a variable usually represent in algebra?
Solve x + 5 = 12.
What does the graph of a linear equation form?
Which expression is an equation?
Simplify 3x + 2x.
Apply the distributive property: 2(x + 3).
What is the goal when solving an equation?

options.option_text: Turn it into a graph immediately

questions.prompt: In the term 4y, what is 4?
What degree does a quadratic expression have?
Why does algebra matter beyond arithmetic?

options.option_text: It helps model patterns and unknowns in many fields
It is used only in geometry proofs
It replaces all later math topics

tests.title: Functions Mastery Test
tests.instructions: Answer questions about the core concepts, interpretations, and applications for this node.

questions.prompt: What is a function in mathematics?

options.option_text: A rule assigning exactly one output to each input
A graph with multiple outputs for one input

questions.prompt: What is the domain of a function?
What is the range of a function?
Which relation is a function?

options.option_text: A circle centered at the origin

questions.prompt: Which test is commonly used on a graph to check if it is a function?
If f(x) = 2x + 1, what is f(3)?
What idea is central to every function?

options.option_text: Outputs depend on inputs according to a rule

questions.prompt: What does the notation f(x) mean?

options.option_text: The output of function f at input x

questions.prompt: What is true of a linear function?

options.option_text: It has a constant rate of change

questions.prompt: Why are functions fundamental in this project?

options.option_text: They power mathematical models used in later topics

tests.title: Trigonometry Mastery Test
tests.instructions: Answer questions about the core concepts, interpretations, and applications for this node.

questions.prompt: What kind of triangle is most directly associated with basic trigonometric ratios?
In SOHCAHTOA, what does sine equal?
What is cos(0)?
What does tangent of an angle equal in a right triangle?
How many radians are equal to 180 degrees?
What point on the unit circle corresponds to 90 degrees?
Which real-world phenomenon is often modeled with trigonometric functions?
What is an angle of elevation measured from?

options.option_text: The center of a circle only

questions.prompt: Which identity is fundamental in trigonometry?
Why does trigonometry matter later in the graph?

options.option_text: It supports geometry, vectors, and calculus applications
It is only useful for counting
It eliminates the need for functions

tests.title: Vectors Mastery Test
tests.instructions: Answer questions about the core concepts, interpretations, and applications for this node.

questions.prompt: What distinguishes a vector from a scalar?

options.option_text: A vector has magnitude and direction

questions.prompt: How are vectors often added geometrically?
What type of quantity is the dot product of two vectors?
Which of these is a vector quantity?
What are the components of the vector (2, 5)?
What is the magnitude of the vector (3, 4)?
What vector points in the exact opposite direction of v?
Why are vectors important in science?

options.option_text: They model directed quantities like force and motion

questions.prompt: What is a unit vector?

options.option_text: A vector with two components only
A vector that cannot be scaled

questions.prompt: In three dimensions, what does the cross product produce?

options.option_text: A vector perpendicular to both inputs

tests.title: Limits Mastery Test
tests.instructions: Answer questions about the core concepts, interpretations, and applications for this node.

questions.prompt: What does a limit describe?

options.option_text: The value a function approaches near a point
The exact slope at every point
The total area under a curve

questions.prompt: What notation is commonly used for a limit?

options.option_text: lim as x approaches a value
integral from a to b only

questions.prompt: When is a function continuous at x = a?

options.option_text: When the limit exists and equals the function value there
When the function is always increasing
When the graph crosses the x-axis

questions.prompt: What is the limit of 1/x as x goes to infinity?
What are one-sided limits?

options.option_text: Limits taken from the left or right side separately

questions.prompt: What kind of discontinuity creates a hole that can sometimes be filled?
Why are limits important in calculus?

options.option_text: They define derivatives and integrals rigorously
They are used only for statistics
They remove the need for functions

questions.prompt: If the left-hand and right-hand limits are different, what happens to the two-sided limit?

options.option_text: It equals the function value automatically

questions.prompt: When can direct substitution often be used to evaluate a limit?

options.option_text: When the function is continuous at the point
When the denominator is always zero
When the function is undefined everywhere

questions.prompt: What is the limit of x^2 as x approaches 2?

tests.title: Derivatives Mastery Test
tests.instructions: Answer questions about the core concepts, interpretations, and applications for this node.

questions.prompt: What does a derivative measure?
What is the derivative of x^2?
What geometric quantity does a derivative give on a graph?
What is the derivative of a constant?
What does the power rule say for x^n?
If a derivative is positive on an interval, what is the function doing there?
In physics, what is the derivative of position with respect to time?
What often marks a critical point of a smooth function?
What is the derivative of 3x + 1?
Why do derivatives matter in applications?

options.option_text: They let us model change, motion, and optimization
They are used only in geometry
They remove the need for limits

tests.title: Integration Mastery Test
tests.instructions: Answer questions about the core concepts, interpretations, and applications for this node.

questions.prompt: What is integration mainly associated with?
What is an antiderivative of 2x?
What makes an integral definite?

options.option_text: It has bounds that specify an interval

questions.prompt: What theorem directly connects derivatives and integrals?
Why is +C included in an indefinite integral?

options.option_text: Antiderivatives can differ by a constant
It means the integral is wrong

questions.prompt: What does a definite integral often represent on a graph?

options.option_text: Signed area under a curve over an interval

questions.prompt: What is the integral of 5 with respect to x?
When is substitution especially useful in integration?

options.option_text: When a composition of functions appears

questions.prompt: What can happen when part of a graph lies below the x-axis in a definite integral?

options.option_text: It forces the integral to be undefined

questions.prompt: Which quantity can often be recovered by integrating velocity?

tests.title: Matrices Mastery Test
tests.instructions: Answer questions about the core concepts, interpretations, and applications for this node.

questions.prompt: What is a matrix?
What are the dimensions of a matrix described by 2 x 3?
What role does the identity matrix play?

options.option_text: It acts like the multiplicative identity
It is used only for addition

questions.prompt: When is matrix multiplication AB defined?

options.option_text: When the columns of A equal the rows of B
When both matrices have the same determinant
When A and B have the same number of rows

questions.prompt: What kind of value is associated with a square matrix and helps indicate invertibility?
When does a square matrix have an inverse?

options.option_text: When it has more rows than columns
When all entries are the same

questions.prompt: How are matrices commonly used with linear systems?

options.option_text: They organize coefficients and constants efficiently
They remove the need for algebra

questions.prompt: How many entries are in a 2 x 3 matrix?
When can two matrices be added?

options.option_text: When they have the same dimensions

questions.prompt: Which applied field relies heavily on matrices?

tests.title: Advanced Linear Algebra Mastery Test
tests.instructions: Answer questions about the core concepts, interpretations, and applications for this node.

questions.prompt: What is an eigenvector?

options.option_text: A nonzero vector whose direction is unchanged by a transformation
A vector with length one only

questions.prompt: What is an eigenvalue?

options.option_text: The factor by which an eigenvector is scaled
The sum of every matrix entry
A vector perpendicular to all others

questions.prompt: What is a basis of a vector space?

options.option_text: A linearly independent set that spans the space
A set of dependent vectors only
A square matrix with determinant zero

questions.prompt: What does the dimension of a vector space count?

options.option_text: The number of vectors in any basis
The number of rows in every matrix
The number of subjects in the graph

questions.prompt: Why is diagonalization useful?

options.option_text: It guarantees every matrix is invertible
It only applies to 2 x 2 matrices

questions.prompt: What is true of orthogonal vectors?

options.option_text: They point in the same direction

questions.prompt: What is a linear transformation?

options.option_text: A map that preserves vector addition and scalar multiplication

questions.prompt: What is the null space of a matrix?

options.option_text: The set of vectors mapped to zero

questions.prompt: What does the rank of a matrix measure?

options.option_text: The number of linearly independent rows or columns
The largest entry in the matrix

questions.prompt: Why does advanced linear algebra matter beyond basic matrices?

options.option_text: It explains structure, transformations, and higher-dimensional systems
It removes the need for calculus

tests.title: Differential Equations Mastery Test
tests.instructions: Answer questions about the core concepts, interpretations, and applications for this node.

questions.prompt: What makes an equation a differential equation?

options.option_text: It contains derivatives of an unknown function

questions.prompt: What kind of object is usually the solution to a differential equation?
What does first-order mean in a differential equation?

options.option_text: The highest derivative is the first derivative
The equation has one variable only
It is the easiest possible equation

questions.prompt: What is the purpose of an initial condition?

options.option_text: It selects a specific solution from a family of solutions

questions.prompt: Which pattern is commonly modeled by y' = ky?
When is separation of variables a useful method?

options.option_text: When the equation can be rewritten with x and y parts separated
When the equation has no derivatives

questions.prompt: Which physical system is often described by a second-order differential equation?
What does a slope field visualize?

options.option_text: The local direction of solution curves

questions.prompt: Why are differential equations powerful in applications?

options.option_text: They model how systems change over time
They remove the need for functions

questions.prompt: Which existing math topics feed directly into differential equations in this graph?

tests.title: Multivariable Calculus Mastery Test
tests.instructions: Answer questions about the core concepts, interpretations, and applications for this node.

questions.prompt: What does multivariable calculus study?

options.option_text: Functions of more than one variable

questions.prompt: What does a partial derivative measure?

options.option_text: Rate of change with respect to one variable while others are fixed

questions.prompt: What does the gradient vector point toward?
What does a double integral usually accumulate over?
What is a level curve?

options.option_text: A set of points where the function has the same value
A tangent line with zero slope

questions.prompt: What does a directional derivative measure?

options.option_text: Rate of change in a chosen direction

questions.prompt: What method is used for optimization with a constraint?
What does a Jacobian help describe?

options.option_text: Local scaling and change of variables

questions.prompt: Which idea from single-variable calculus extends into multivariable calculus?

options.option_text: It supports optimization, fields, and multi-input models

tests.title: Real Analysis Mastery Test
tests.instructions: Answer questions about the core concepts, interpretations, and applications for this node.

questions.prompt: What is real analysis mainly concerned with?

options.option_text: The rigorous foundations of calculus on the real numbers

questions.prompt: What does an epsilon-delta definition formalize?

options.option_text: What it means for a limit to exist

questions.prompt: What does it mean for a sequence to converge?

options.option_text: Its terms get arbitrarily close to a limit

questions.prompt: What is a Cauchy sequence?

options.option_text: A sequence whose terms become arbitrarily close to each other
A sequence with constant difference always
A sequence with only positive terms

questions.prompt: Which property of the real numbers is central in analysis?
What is uniform continuity stronger than?

options.option_text: The subject emphasizes precise justification over intuition alone

questions.prompt: What is the supremum of a set?
Why does real analysis matter later in mathematics?

options.option_text: It makes calculus-based reasoning rigorous and trustworthy
It matters only for geometry coloring

tests.title: Abstract Algebra Mastery Test
tests.instructions: Answer questions about the core concepts, interpretations, and applications for this node.

questions.prompt: What does abstract algebra study?

options.option_text: Structures such as groups, rings, and fields

questions.prompt: What is a group?

options.option_text: A set with an operation satisfying specific axioms

questions.prompt: What does an identity element do?

options.option_text: Leaves other elements unchanged under the operation

questions.prompt: What does an inverse element do?

options.option_text: Undoes the operation relative to the identity

questions.prompt: Which set forms a group under addition?
What does it mean for a group to be commutative?

options.option_text: Order does not affect the result of the operation
Every element is its own inverse
The group has exactly two elements

questions.prompt: What structure has two compatible operations, often addition and multiplication?
What extra property makes a field stronger than a ring?
What is a homomorphism?
Why is abstract algebra important outside pure math?

options.option_text: It underpins cryptography, symmetry, and coding theory
It removes the need for functions

tests.title: Number Line Integers Mastery Test
tests.instructions: Answer questions about the core concepts, interpretations, and applications for this node.

questions.prompt: What does the number line help visualize?

questions.prompt: Which set is represented by integers?

options.option_text: Negative whole numbers, zero, and positive whole numbers

questions.prompt: What is the absolute value of -7?

What is 4 - (-2)?
How far apart are -2 and 3 on the number line?

options.option_text: It is neither positive nor negative

questions.prompt: Why is the number line useful before algebra?

options.option_text: It builds intuition about order, direction, and signed values

tests.title: Order Of Operations Mastery Test

questions.prompt: Which part of PEMDAS/BODMAS is handled first?

options.option_text: At the same time as addition

questions.prompt: How are multiplication and division handled if they appear together?

What is 2^3 + 1?
Why do we use a fixed order of operations?
Which topic benefits directly from mastering order of operations?

tests.title: Decimals Percentages Mastery Test
tests.instructions: Answer questions about the core concepts, interpretations, and applications for this node.

questions.prompt: What percentage is equal to 0.5?

tests.title: Coordinate Geometry Mastery Test
tests.instructions: Answer questions about the core concepts, interpretations, and applications for this node.

questions.prompt: What does an ordered pair (x, y) represent?

options.option_text: A point in the coordinate plane

questions.prompt: Which axis is horizontal?

tests.title: Polynomials Mastery Test
tests.instructions: Answer questions about the core concepts, interpretations, and applications for this node.

questions.prompt: What is a polynomial?

options.option_text: An expression made from terms with nonnegative integer exponents

questions.prompt: What is the degree of a polynomial?

options.option_text: The highest exponent on its variable
